generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building control actuator for adaptive lighting systems in BOREALIS, implementing reinforcement learning-based optimization for energy efficiency and visual comfort"
]

instance EnergyOptimizationActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator for energy consumption optimization in LEED Platinum academic building, integrating with Eclipse Hono for real-time control signals"
]

instance ReinforcementLearningController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Domain-specific controller implementing reinforcement learning algorithms for adaptive lighting strategies in BOREALIS"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy sensor tracking building occupancy levels for adaptive lighting control in BIM-based DT"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataStream
]

instance LightingLevelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring real-time lighting levels to validate BIM-based control strategies in DT"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightingLevelDataStream
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy energy consumption sensor feeding operational data into DT for predictive analytics"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataStream
]

instance NonVisualHealthSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring non-visual health effects (e.g., circadian rhythms) for adaptive lighting optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData NonVisualHealthDataStream
]

instance BIMConfigurationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Model-driven sensor representing BIM-enriched device configurations for control logic validation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData BIMConfigurationMetadata
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance OccupancyDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance LightingLevelDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightingLevelSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance NonVisualHealthDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom NonVisualHealthSensor
]

instance BIMConfigurationMetadata : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BIMConfigurationSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMEnrichedModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static 3D BIM model enriched with runtime semantics (sensor/actuator configurations) for DT deployment in LEED Platinum academic building"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMTooling
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineering
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational data stream from post-occupancy sensors (occupancy, lighting levels, energy consumption) for DT synchronization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataStream
    DTDFVocab:fromData LightingLevelDataStream
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyConsumptionDataStream
    DTDFVocab:fromData NonVisualHealthDataStream
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Domain-specific control strategies (e.g., adaptive lighting algorithms) embedded as services within DT framework for reinforcement learning-based optimization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineering
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHono
]

instance BIMConfigurationMetadataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Metadata extracted from BIM models representing device configurations and middleware endpoints for DT deployment"
    DTDFVocab:fromData BIMConfigurationMetadata
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelDrivenEngineering
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHono
]

instance AdaptiveLightingPolicy : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Actionable control policies generated by reinforcement learning for adaptive lighting optimization in BOREALIS"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RealTimeOperationalData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ReinforcementLearningController
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHono
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BIMTooling : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Autodesk Revit or similar BIM platform for modeling static and dynamic building control system components in LEED Platinum academic building"
    DTDFVocab:enables BIMEnrichedModel
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineering : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework automating extraction of BIM semantics and generation of runtime interfaces via model-to-model/model-to-text transformations"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlLogicModel
    DTDFVocab:enables BIMConfigurationMetadataModel
]

instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware platform enabling real-time bidirectional data exchange between physical building control systems and DT artifacts"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeOperationalData
    DTDFVocab:enables BIMConfigurationMetadataModel
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveLightingPolicy
]

instance UnrealEngine5 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D visualization platform for interactive BIM-based DT monitoring and real-time synchronization with physical building systems"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeOperationalData
]

instance StandardizedExtensionPoints : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Modular architecture allowing DT Engineers to deploy domain-specific plugins (e.g., IoT device management, analytics) without redeploying entire system"
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time monitoring and visualization of BIM-based DT via Unreal Engine 5 for immersive 3D interaction with building control systems"
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveVisualizationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeStateInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing predictive analytics for adaptive lighting optimization using reinforcement learning in BOREALIS, balancing energy consumption, visual comfort, and non-visual health effects"
    DTDFVocab:provides AdaptiveLightingPolicy
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationInsight
]

instance AutomatedCommissioningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service automating bidirectional communication between physical building control devices and DT artifacts via Eclipse Hono/Ditto middleware"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlSignalAction
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationAction
]

instance CustomServiceIntegration : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling domain-specific plugin integration through standardized extension points for IoT device management and analytics"
    DTDFVocab:provides PluginDeploymentAction
    DTDFVocab:provides AnalyticsExtension
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ImmersiveVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D visualization of BIM-based DT state for facility managers to monitor occupancy trends, energy usage, and building performance in real-time"
]

instance RealTimeStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Proactive identification of building inefficiencies (energy waste, occupancy mismatches) via real-time data streams for automated or manual interventions"
]

instance AdaptiveLightingPolicy : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Reinforcement learning-generated control policies dynamically adjusting lighting levels based on occupancy, energy consumption, and non-visual health metrics"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Performance metrics showing energy consumption patterns and optimization opportunities for LEED Platinum building operations"
]

instance ControlSignalAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated actuation commands sent via Eclipse Hono to physical lighting actuators based on DT-generated control strategies"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ValidationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Semantic validation of BIM models before transformation to ensure consistency with physical building control systems"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PluginDeploymentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Incremental upgrade of DT services through standardized extension points without redeploying entire system infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnalyticsExtension : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Deployment of domain-specific analytics plugins (e.g., IoT device management) to extend DT decision-making capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C17: Environment and Component relationships
instance AcademicBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LightingActuator
    base:contains EnergyOptimizationActuator
    base:contains ReinforcementLearningController
    base:contains OccupancySensor
    base:contains LightingLevelSensor
    base:contains EnergyConsumptionSensor
    base:contains NonVisualHealthSensor
    base:contains BIMConfigurationSensor
    base:contains BIMEnrichedModel
    base:contains RealTimeOperationalData
]

instance LEEDPlatinumBuildingComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "LEED Platinum academic building infrastructure system integrating BIM models with real-time operational data for end-to-end DT deployment"
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment AcademicBuildingEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Virtual-to-physical interaction is facilitated by **service-oriented control logic** embedded in the DT, which generates control signals to actuate physical devices. The framework uses **standardized extension points** to integrate custom services (e.g., DT engineers’ plugins) and middleware (Eclipse Hono/Ditto) to send commands back to physical systems. This enables closed-loop control, such as adaptive lighting adjustments based on occupancy and energy consumption data, ensuring seamless synchronization between the DT and operational building systems."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on a **real-time to near-real-time** time scale, with synchronization rates determined by sensor data acquisition frequencies. For adaptive lighting systems (e.g., BOREALIS), the framework supports **iterative, closed-loop control** where DT-generated control policies are refined based on post-occupancy sensor feedback (e.g., every few minutes to hours, depending on system requirements). The overall DT-to-physical synchronization adheres to the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, ensuring high fidelity between virtual and physical representations."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **service-oriented, layered system** with the following components:
  1. **BIM Backbone**: Static 3D spatial and functional data from BIM models, enriched with runtime semantics (e.g., sensor/actuator configurations).
  2. **Runtime Middleware Layer**: **Eclipse Hono/Ditto** handles real-time data synchronization between physical and virtual assets.
  3. **Control Logic Layer**: Domain-specific services (e.g., adaptive lighting, energy optimization) implemented as plugins or custom services, integrated via standardized extension points.
  4. **Visualization Layer**: **Unreal Engine 5** provides interactive 3D interfaces for monitoring and control.
  The constellation ensures **interoperability** via ODTE metrics and **scalability** through modular, service-oriented design."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans the following life cycle stages of the physical building:
  1. **Design Phase**: Enriched BIM models incorporate static and dynamic BCS components (e.g., sensors, actuators) for initial DT modeling.
  2. **Construction Phase**: The framework automates the extraction of deployment metadata (e.g., device IDs, middleware configurations) from BIM to generate runtime artifacts for DT deployment.
  3. **Operation Phase**: Real-time synchronization occurs via Eclipse Hono/Ditto, enabling adaptive control (e.g., BOREALIS’s reinforcement learning-based lighting optimization) and monitoring via Unreal Engine.
  4. **Refurbishment Phase**: The modular architecture supports runtime configuration management, allowing DT services to evolve without full redeployment (e.g., adding new sensors or control logic)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  1. **Model-Driven Transformation**: Automated extraction of BIM semantics (e.g., device metadata) to generate consistent runtime artifacts, reducing manual errors.
  2. **Real-Time Synchronization**: **Eclipse Hono/Ditto** middleware guarantees live data exchange between physical and virtual assets, minimizing latency and drift.
  3. **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) Metric**: Quantifies the alignment between the DT and its physical counterpart, ensuring high fidelity across the lifecycle.
  4. **Runtime Configuration Management**: Allows dynamic adjustments to DT services (e.g., control logic) without full redeployment, preserving validity during evolution.
  5. **Verification/Validation Mechanisms**: Implicitly validated via the Living Lab case study, where DT performance is measured against physical building operational data."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT technical connection relies on:
  1. **Network Protocols**: **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto** use standardized IoT protocols (e.g., MQTT, CoAP) for real-time data exchange between physical sensors/actuators and the DT.
  2. **Middleware Architecture**: A layered system with:
     - **BIM-to-Runtime Interface**: Model-driven transformations extract BIM semantics for DT deployment.
     - **Physical-to-Virtual Sync**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto handles bidirectional data streams (e.g., sensor readings → DT, DT commands → actuators).
     - **Visualization Layer**: Unreal Engine 5 connects to the DT via WebSocket or REST APIs for interactive monitoring.
  3. **Scalability**: The modular architecture supports horizontal scaling of DT services (e.g., adding more sensors or control logic) without network bottlenecks."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted and deployed via:
  1. **Cloud/Edge Deployment**: Middleware (Eclipse Hono/Ditto) can run on edge devices (e.g., IoT gateways) or cloud-based platforms for scalability.
  2. **Unreal Engine 5**: The visualization layer is deployed as a web-based or standalone application, accessible via a browser or dedicated client.
  3. **Standardized Extension Points**: DT services (e.g., custom plugins) are deployed incrementally, leveraging the framework’s modular architecture to avoid redeployment.
  4. **BIM Integration**: The DT is tightly coupled with BIM models via model-driven transformations, ensuring seamless alignment between design-time and runtime artifacts."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration occurs via:
  1. **BIM-to-DT Pipeline**: Automated model-driven transformations extract BIM semantics (e.g., device metadata) for DT deployment, ensuring consistency across AECO stakeholders (e.g., architects, engineers, facility managers).
  2. **Middleware Interoperability**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto supports bidirectional communication with external IoT platforms (e.g., non-BIM-based sensors) via standardized protocols (e.g., MQTT).
  3. **Service-Oriented Architecture**: Standardized extension points enable integration with third-party analytics tools or control systems (e.g., building management systems) without proprietary dependencies.
  4. **Living Lab Case Study**: The framework demonstrates horizontal integration by connecting BIM-based DTs with real-world operational data (e.g., post-occupancy sensor feedback) to validate scalability."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership and privacy are addressed as follows:
  1. **Ownership**: Data is primarily owned by the **physical building owner** (e.g., university/LEED Platinum building), with DT services acting as intermediaries for processing and visualization.
  2. **Privacy**: The framework does not explicitly mention proprietary data handling, but assumes compliance with AECO standards (e.g., BIM data sharing protocols) and middleware security (e.g., TLS encryption for Eclipse Hono/Ditto).
  3. **Access Control**: DT services (e.g., visualization via Unreal Engine) are designed for authorized stakeholders (e.g., Facility Managers, DT Engineers) via role-based access controls (RAC), though specifics are not detailed in the documents."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to the following standards:
  1. **BIM Standards**: Building Information Modeling (BIM) as the semantic backbone for spatial and functional data representation.
  2. **IoT Protocols**: **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto** leverage standardized IoT protocols (e.g., MQTT, CoAP) for real-time data exchange.
  3. **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)**: Follows MDE principles for model-to-model/model-to-text transformations to automate DT deployment from BIM.
  4. **AECO-Specific Frameworks**: Supports **TwinLight** (BIM-centric control configurations) and **BOREALIS** (adaptive lighting with reinforcement learning) as domain-specific extensions.
  5. **ODTE Metric**: Uses the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE)** metric to quantify interoperability and architectural soundness."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
  1. **Network Security**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto middleware uses **TLS encryption** and standardized IoT protocols (e.g., MQTT over WebSockets) to secure data in transit.
  2. **Access Control**: Role-based access controls (RAC) restrict DT interactions to authorized stakeholders (e.g., Facility Managers, DT Engineers) via Unreal Engine or middleware APIs.
  3. **Physical Safety**: The framework does not explicitly mention remote execution of critical operations on physical assets, but assumes compliance with AECO safety protocols (e.g., manual overrides for actuators) to prevent unintended accidents.
  4. **Runtime Validation**: The ODTE metric ensures the DT’s fidelity to the physical system, reducing risks of misaligned control signals.
  5. **Modularity**: Standardized extension points minimize exposure of core DT components to external threats by isolating custom services."
]
